title: DecantCC case questions
description: Question set for one corpus case (see corpus/README.md). Gold answers come from the SOURCE document, never from a conversion.

defaultSnippets.label: numeric question
defaultSnippets.description: Graded by first committed number vs gold within tolerance

defaultSnippets.label: exact question
defaultSnippets.description: Normalized equality / barely-longer containment; judge fallback

defaultSnippets.label: set question
defaultSnippets.description: Fraction of gold anchors present as whole words, any order

defaultSnippets.label: ordered_list question
defaultSnippets.description: Like set, but anchors must appear in sequence

body.question: ${2:In order, what are the steps of ...?}

defaultSnippets.label: open question
defaultSnippets.description: Free-form, graded by LLM judge against the gold summary

body.question: ${2:In one or two sentences, ...?}
body.gold: ${3:A model answer the judge compares against.}

id.description: Kebab-case slug, unique within the case.

question.description: The question as posed to the model. Anchor it to the document (e.g. 'According to the FAQ...') so it can't be answered from world knowledge.

type.description: numeric: first committed number vs gold within tolerance (spelled-out numbers count when no digit). exact: normalized equality / barely-longer containment, judge fallback. set: fraction of gold items present as whole words. ordered_list: like set but in sequence. open: LLM judge.

gold.description: The gold answer. String or number for numeric/exact/open; array of string anchors for set/ordered_list. Grader-aware: avoid space-separated thousands ('28 215') and hyphen/diacritic-fragile exact strings; prefer short anchors over long verbatim phrases.

tolerance.description: numeric only: absolute tolerance on the value.

source.description: Where in the source document the answer lives ('figure-9', 'table-3', 'text') - free-form, case-scoped. Enables per-source report slicing; tag figure-borne questions so companion-subset arms can be compared.

gold.description: numeric gold must parse as a number (digits, optional thousands commas and decimal).
set/ordered_list gold is an array of short string anchors.
